title: Story 2.3: Event Detail & Tool Input View
description: As a Claude Code developer, I want to expand any event to see its full stdin payload and tool input, so that I can understand exactly what data the agent sent.

title: Implement client broadcast in `src/server/stream.ts` — export a `broadcast(event)` function that iterates over all connected SSE clients and writes the event JSON (AC: #1)
Phase 1: Design & PRD
description: Use BMAD plugin to produce PRD, break v0 scope into epics and stories. All design artifacts finalized before implementation begins.
close_reason: Phase 1 complete: product brief, PRD, architecture, and epics/stories all done

title: Create `src/cli/install.ts` — generate plugin files, call `claude plugin install`, handle reinstall by uninstalling first (AC: #1, #2)
Run Biome lint + `bun test` to verify (AC: #1, #2, #3)
Phase 2: Implementation
description: Orchestrated execution of v0 stories. Each story includes its own unit tests. Implementation follows the story order from Phase 1.

title: Update design doc: Preact + htm replaces Svelte for web UI
description: Svelte requires a build step (conflicts with NFR-5). Preact + htm works without a build pipeline using tagged template literals (~3KB). Update FR-4 and Key Decisions in design doc.

title: Detect platform for browser open command — use `process.platform` to choose between `open` (darwin) and `xdg-open` (linux) (AC: #1, #2)
Evaluate OTEL backend export
description: Decide whether hookwatch should support exporting collected data to OTEL backends (Grafana, Datadog, etc.). Not hook-based — would be an optional export layer on top of SQLite. Low priority, likely not needed.

title: Create `biome.json` with lint + format configuration (AC: #1)
Story 2.1: Serve Web UI & Event List Page
description: As a Claude Code developer, I want to open a browser and see a chronological list of hook events, so that I can review what the agent did.

title: Create `src/server/stream.ts` — SSE endpoint handler for `GET /api/events/stream`, maintains a `Set` of connected response writers, sends `data: {json}\n\n` format (AC: #1)
Implement JSON formatting — use `JSON.stringify(payload, null, 2)` rendered inside `<pre><code>` tags for formatted display (AC: #1)
Display original command as formatted text (e.g., my-hook-script.sh arg1 arg2) above the I/O panels (AC: #2)
Create `src/cli/open.ts` — citty subcommand definition for `hookwatch open` with `--port` flag (number type, optional) (AC: #1, #3)
Test Claude Code behavior with non-JSON hook stdout
description: Verify what happens when a hook writes non-JSON content to stdout before/after JSON output. Does Claude Code: (a) fail to parse, (b) find the JSON, or (c) silently ignore? This determines whether hookwatch handler needs strict stdout discipline (no console.log, redirect subprocess stdout). Test during implementation.

title: On health timeout (2s), exit 1 — non-blocking error, no retry loop (AC: #4)
Ensure JSON content is rendered as text content (not HTML) — use Preact's text interpolation, NEVER `innerHTML` (AC: #1)
Validate hook stdout output against Claude Code SDK schema
description: hookwatch should validate hook stdout (output to Claude Code) in addition to stdin (input from Claude Code). If the JSON output doesn't conform to the expected format (continue, systemMessage, hookSpecificOutput, etc.), log a warning in the web UI. This catches silent failures where Claude Code ignores malformed hook output. Especially important for hookwatch wrap where wrapped commands may leak stdout and corrupt the JSON. Zod schemas for output derived from Claude Code SDK alongside input schemas.

title: Consider splitting wrap captures to separate table if performance issues arise
description: v0 stores wrap data (stdout, stderr, exit_code) in the same events table. If wrap outputs are large and cause performance issues with event list queries, split into a dedicated wraps table with foreign key to events. Only act on this if real performance problems are observed.

title: Story 4.2: Handler Context Injection Output
description: As a Claude Code developer, I want hookwatch to output structured system messages to Claude Code, so that the agent receives context about captured events.

title: Update design doc: three interfaces (web UI, SQLite, skills)
description: Design doc currently only describes SQLite and web UI. Add the third interface: Claude Code skills shipped with the plugin (v1) for agent-queryable access to hookwatch data. Also add CLI wrap as part of the backend.

title: Write integration test — verify SSE respects session filter: ingest event with non-matching session, confirm it does not appear (AC: #3)
Add query helpers in `src/db/queries.ts` — parameterized SELECT for events with optional filters, ORDER BY ts DESC (AC: #2)
Extend `src/schemas/query.ts` — add optional `session_id` field to the filter schema and add `queryType` discriminator field (values: `"events"` | `"sessions"`) (AC: #1, #2)
Implement child process spawning with piped stdio — use Bun.spawn() with stdin: "pipe", stdout: "pipe", stderr: "pipe" (AC: #1)
Create `src/cli/generate.ts` — generate `plugin.json` and `hooks.json` from source (AC: #1)
Benchmark Bun cold start: handler.ts without daemon
description: Before committing to daemon architecture, benchmark bun run handler.ts cold start (process spawn + SQLite open + Zod load + write + exit). If <100ms is achievable without a daemon, simpler architecture is possible. Run early in implementation.

title: Use collapsible panels — <details> or <article> elements via Pico CSS for each I/O stream (AC: #2)
Create `README.md` with one-liner description, install command, and dev commands (AC: #1)
Call `.unref()` on the child process handle immediately after spawn so the handler can exit without waiting for the server (AC: #1)
Upstream architecture: observability uses manual server start + HTTP POST from hooks
description: Research finding from Advanced Elicitation. The observability upstream uses: manual bun run dev to start server, Python hooks send HTTP POST to localhost:4000/events, WebSocket broadcast to Vue client, silent failure if server not running (events lost). hookwatch improves on this with auto-start daemon. Reference: .git-ignored/upstream/claude-code-hooks-multi-agent-observability/

title: Implement tool-related event detection — check if `event` field is one of: `PreToolUse`, `PostToolUse`, `PostToolUseFailure`, `PermissionRequest` (AC: #2)
Wait for child process exit — capture exit code from child process (AC: #2)
Write unit test for explicit port error — verify error message and exit code when port is occupied (AC: #4)
Run Biome lint + bun test to verify (Story 1.3)
Update `src/ui/events/event-list.ts` — subscribe to `activeSession` signal, re-fetch events via `POST /api/query { "filter": { "session_id": "..." } }` when the signal changes (AC: #2, #3)
Create `src/server/index.ts` — `Bun.serve()` setup, route dispatch, bind to `127.0.0.1` (AC: #1)
Story 3.2: Wrap I/O Viewer in Web UI
description: As a hook author, I want to see captured wrap I/O in the web UI, so that I can debug my hook's input/output behavior visually.

title: Construct `systemMessage` identifying source hook name and event type from parsed stdin payload (AC: #1)
Handle EventSource reconnection — `EventSource` auto-reconnects by default; verify no custom logic overrides this behavior (AC: #2)
Story 1.6: Plugin Manifest & CLI Install/Uninstall
description: As a Claude Code developer, I want to install hookwatch with a single command, so that all 18 hook event types are registered automatically.

title: Add timing measurement — log if handler exceeds 100ms threshold (AC: #2)
Phase 4: Release
description: Release preparation for v0.1.0. GitHub remote setup, packaging, docs finalization, version tagging, installation verification.

title: Story 2.2: Session Filter
description: As a Claude Code developer, I want to filter events by session ID, so that I can focus on what happened in a specific session.

title: Create `src/db/schema.ts` — CREATE TABLE events DDL, `PRAGMA user_version` migration check (AC: #2)
Story 1.1: Project Initialization & SQLite Database Layer
description: As a developer building hookwatch, I want a project skeleton with a SQLite database layer, so that events have a storage foundation to write to.

title: Create discriminated parse function that routes by `hook_event_name` to the correct schema (AC: #1, #2)
Fix docs: hook_duration_ms tracks OUR handler time, not Claude Code tool duration
description: README, design doc, and product brief incorrectly imply we can measure how long Claude Code's tools take. We only measure how long our handler.ts takes to execute. hook_duration_ms = time from handler start to handler exit. Update all references to be precise about what we measure.

title: Redirect spawned server stdout/stderr to a log file (e.g., `~/.local/share/hookwatch/server.log`) (AC: #1)
Document data sensitivity risk and set file permissions
description: v0: hookwatch stores all hook stdin payloads in plaintext SQLite, including potential API keys, credentials, and secrets from tool_input. Actions: (1) README section warning users about plaintext storage, (2) create database file with 0600 permissions (owner-only), (3) suggest full-disk encryption for sensitive environments. No runtime encryption in v0 — bun:sqlite doesn't support SQLCipher without custom build.

title: Write unit test for session_id query helper — verify DISTINCT query returns unique session IDs (AC: #1)
Story 4.1: Hook Stdout Validation Schemas
description: As a hookwatch developer, I want Zod schemas for hook stdout output, so that messages sent to Claude Code are validated before delivery.

title: Verify `bun test` passes and `bun install` succeeds from clean state (AC: #1, #2, #3)
Create `src/schemas/events.test.ts` with tests for each event type, unknown event handling, and missing field rejection (AC: #1, #2, #3)
POST wrap event to /api/events with event: "Wrap" type after child process completes (AC: #1, #2)
Create `src/cli/index.ts` — citty main entrypoint, register subcommands (install, uninstall, open, wrap), global flags (--help, --version) (AC: #4)
Define `plugin.json` structure with name, version (from package.json), and author (AC: #1)
Create `.gitignore` with node_modules, *.db, .env, dist entries (AC: #1)
Architectural decision: persistent backend daemon (auto-started by handler)
description: hookwatch needs a persistent Bun daemon process. Handler auto-starts it on first hook invocation if not running. Daemon manages: SQLite writes, web UI server, WebSocket broadcast, wrap CLI. Self-terminates after inactivity. This resolves cold start performance (NFR-1), hook-server communication, and multi-plugin shared state. Update design doc with this decision.
notes: Idle timeout: daemon self-terminates after ~30-60 min of inactivity (no hook events, no web UI connections). Next hook invocation restarts it. No data loss — SQLite is the source of truth, not in-memory state. Use WAL mode for concurrent access.

title: Update design doc: lead with outcomes, not event counts
description: Vision should lead with 'see what Claude did, debug your hooks, query your sessions' rather than '18 event types'. The count is infrastructure, not the value prop. Still mention 'all 18' but not as the headline.

title: Validate parsed JSON with Zod schema from `@/schemas/events` — use discriminated parse by `hook_event_name` (AC: #1, #4)
On POST failure (server unreachable after spawn+retry): exit 1, write nothing to stdout (AC: #2)
Handle "All sessions" selection — set `activeSession` signal to `null` or empty, fetch without session_id filter (AC: #3)
Add `bin` field to `package.json` pointing to CLI entry point (AC: #1, #4)
Create `src/handler/spawn.ts` — spawn logic + health probe (AC: #1, #2)
Restore hook chaining (chain_and_exit) dropped in marker migration
description: Gen 1 (bd-shim v1, v0.48-v0.49) had chain_and_exit() which forwarded to .git/hooks/<name> after running beads logic. This is critical because core.hooksPath completely overrides .git/hooks/. Gen 2+ (v0.57+, section markers) dropped this. Repos with their own .git/hooks/pre-commit etc. have those hooks silently ignored. Restore chaining: run .old file if present, then rename .old to .old.backup. Reference: ~/base/manifold/beads-testing/25-git-hook-migration-issue.md section 'Additional Observation: Lost Local Hook Chaining'

title: Create example hooks and demo scenarios
description: Build a set of example hooks and demo scenarios that showcase hookwatch's capabilities. Examples: (1) basic event browsing demo, (2) hookwatch wrap debugging demo, (3) session filtering demo, (4) context injection demo. These serve as both documentation and validation of the project's value. Include a README with instructions to run each example.

title: Configure `tsconfig.json` with strict mode, path alias `@/` mapping to `./src/`, correct moduleResolution and target (AC: #1)
Homebrew distribution strategy (custom tap or core)
description: hookwatch needs a distribution strategy for Homebrew. Bun is not in homebrew-core (only in oven-sh/bun tap), so hookwatch cannot depends_on bun in core. Options: (1) custom tap (homebrew-hookwatch) with bun dependency, (2) bun build --compile for standalone binary that works in core, (3) wait for Bun to enter core. Also needs ≥75 GitHub stars before core submission. v0 uses claude plugin install only.

title: Create `src/ui/app.ts` — Preact app entry point using htm tagged template literals, define `eventList` signal, render root component, mount to DOM (AC: #1, #2, #3)
Verify SSE live updates include wrap events — new wrap events appear in the list without page refresh (AC: #1)
Extend `src/server/query.ts` — route on `queryType`: when `"sessions"` run DISTINCT session_id query; when `"events"` (default) apply optional `session_id` WHERE clause (AC: #1, #2)
Write integration test — ingest an event via POST while UI is open, verify event appears in list without refresh (AC: #1)
Interesting features for other projects
description: Features from upstream tools that are out of scope for hookwatch but worth exploring in separate projects: I1 proxy-based API interception (dev-agent-lens), C9 risk scoring + posture escalation (M87-Spine-lite), C11 cryptographic receipts (M87-Spine-lite), G1-G8 context/workflow automation (claudekit, conclaude).

title: Enforce plugin.json version matches package.json version
description: Add a script (release or CI check) that verifies plugin.json version matches package.json version. plugin.json is generated from source, so this is a safety check to prevent drift. Post-v0, pre-v1.

title: Build wrap event payload — JSON object with stdout, stderr, exit_code, and original command array in the payload column (AC: #2)
Install dev dependencies: `bun add -d @biomejs/biome bun-types` (AC: #1)
Epic 1: Install & Capture Events
description: Developer installs hookwatch and all 18 hook event types flow into SQLite storage. Verifiable immediately via server logs or direct database query. FRs: FR1-9, FR20, FR23-26. NFRs: NFR1-2, NFR4-8, NFR9-11, NFR12-14. Stories: 1.1, 1.2, 1.3, 1.4, 1.5, 1.6.

title: Run Biome lint + `bun test` to verify (AC: #1, #2, #3, #4)

Create `src/ui/events/event-list.ts` — Preact component that fetches events via `POST /api/query` on mount, renders reverse-chronological list with columns: timestamp, event type, session ID, tool name (AC: #2)
Render tool info header — when event is tool-related, display `tool_name` and `tool_input` prominently above the raw payload using semantic HTML (e.g., `<dl>` definition list) (AC: #2)
Inject hookwatch context via hook stdout messages
description: Use hook stdout to inject system messages into Claude Code conversations. Examples: SessionStart shows hookwatch status + previous session summary, Stop shows session stats. Each message must identify its source hook AND event type (e.g. '[hookwatch:PreToolUse:Bash] ...'). Explore all hook event types for useful context injection. Constraint: messages must be concise and not block normal user experience. Test which fields are most useful. v0 or v1 depending on complexity.

title: Promotion/marketing strategy
description: Plan discovery and promotion strategy for hookwatch. Where to announce, how users find us. Deferred until v1 is complete. Phase: v2.

title: Create src/ui/wrap/wrap-viewer.ts — Preact component for rendering wrap event detail view with two I/O panels (stdout and stderr) (AC: #2)
Rename 'daemon' to 'Bun server' in all docs
description: The persistent background process is a Bun server, not a Unix daemon. Update design doc, product brief, PRD, and README. No fork/setsid/PID file — just a long-running Bun process on localhost. Cross-platform compatible.

title: Implement tee behavior for stdout — read from child stdout stream, write to both process.stdout and accumulate in a buffer (AC: #1)
Implement idle timer in `src/server/index.ts` — create a `setTimeout` that calls the graceful shutdown function after the configured timeout period (AC: #1)
Register wrap subcommand in src/cli/index.ts (AC: #1)
Story 1.4: Hook Handler: Receive, Validate & Forward Events
description: As a Claude Code developer, I want the hook handler to capture every event and forward it to the server, so that my agent activity is recorded without interrupting my workflow.

title: Epic 3: Hook Debugging with Wrap
description: Developer wraps any command to capture its stdin/stdout/stderr and views the captured I/O in the web UI. Core hook debugging workflow. FRs: FR14, FR17-19. Depends on Epic 1, Epic 2. Stories: 3.1, 3.2.

title: Update `src/ui/events/event-list.ts` — make each row clickable, toggle expansion, render `event-detail.ts` when expanded (AC: #1, #3)
Handle spawn race condition: retry POST after failed server spawn
description: When two handlers fire simultaneously during cold start, both detect server is down and try to spawn. One succeeds, one fails. The failing handler should retry the POST (server should be up from the other spawn). v0: retry approach. v1: file lock for proper coordination.

title: Create src/cli/wrap.test.ts — unit tests with mock child process covering tee behavior, exit code capture, event POST, and auto-start fallback (AC: #1, #2, #3)
Add a distinct session_id query helper in `src/db/queries.ts` — parameterized SELECT DISTINCT session_id FROM events ORDER BY ts DESC (AC: #1)
Story 3.1: Wrap Command — Capture & Passthrough I/O
description: As a hook author, I want to wrap any command to capture its stdin, stdout, and stderr, so that I can see exactly what data flows through my hook.

title: Update hook-json-schema.md in agent-console-dashboard
description: Append a line to /Users/pablo/LocalDocs/repo/PabloLION/agent-console-dashboard/.git-ignored/hook-json-schema.md pointing to hookwatch's more complete version (GitHub permalink). Discrepancies: missing ConfigChange, WorktreeCreate, WorktreeRemove events; missing last_assistant_message on Stop and SubagentStop.

title: Shared secret token for handler→server authentication
description: Shared secret token for handler-server auth. Deferred to v5 — any file-readable secret is defeatable by attacker with filesystem access. If attacker has local filesystem access, hookwatch is not the primary threat. Revisit if hookwatch ever serves non-localhost.
notes: v5+ deferral per user decision during Red Team analysis

title: Implement health probe: `GET /health` at 50ms intervals, max 2s (40 attempts) (AC: #2)

Implement graceful shutdown function — close all SSE connections (from `src/server/stream.ts` client set), close the SQLite database connection, log shutdown reason, then call `process.exit(0)` (AC: #1)
Add context injection config option — opt-in via environment variable or config file (AC: #1)
Add wrap event visual indicator to the event list component — different background color or icon to distinguish "Wrap" events from hook events (AC: #1)
Evaluate quality gates (C12)
description: Decide whether hookwatch should enforce tests/lint passing before session close. From M87-Spine-lite. Guardrail category (v3).

title: Decide handler error surfacing strategy (exit 1 vs exit 0 + JSON error)
description: When handler fails to deliver event to server, how should it surface the error? Two options narrowed down: (A) Exit 1 + stderr message — simple but unclear if user sees it. (B) Exit 0 + JSON response with error field — Claude Code parses it but exit 0 implies success. Need to test actual Claude Code behavior with both approaches. Blocked by ch-20v (test non-JSON hook stdout). Only options A and B are acceptable.

title: Write Playwright integration test — verify index.html loads with Pico CSS, event list renders sample events in correct order, empty state displays when no events exist (AC: #1, #2, #3)

Create Playwright browser test for wrap viewer — test visual indicator, panel rendering, exit code display, and session filtering (AC: #1, #2, #3)
Read timeout configuration — load idle timeout value from `~/.config/hookwatch/config.toml` via smol-toml, with a default of 30 minutes if config is missing or unset (AC: #1)
Implement tee behavior for stderr — read from child stderr stream, write to both process.stderr and accumulate in a buffer (AC: #1)

Add SSE client logic in `src/ui/app.ts` or a dedicated `src/ui/shared/sse-client.ts` — create `EventSource` connected to `/api/events/stream`, parse incoming events, push to `eventList` signal (AC: #1, #2)
Read ALL of stdin (Bun stdin API), parse as JSON (AC: #1)
Setup wizard for hookwatch configuration
description: v1: Interactive setup wizard for first-time configuration. Covers: encryption preferences (suggest full-disk encryption), event filtering rules, log retention policy, Bun server port selection. Runs on first hookwatch open or via hookwatch setup command.

title: Wrap entire handler in top-level try/catch — exit 0 on success, exit 1 on ANY error (AC: #3)
Create `src/db/queries.ts` — parameterized INSERT and SELECT helpers for the events table (AC: #2, #3)
hookwatch wrap CLI: tee-like command wrapper for hook debugging
description: A CLI command (hookwatch wrap <command>) that wraps any hook/bash command, duplicating stdin/stdout/stderr to the hookwatch database. Like tee but for all file descriptors. Users can debug their own hooks by seeing exact I/O in the web UI. v1 feature.

title: Run Biome lint + bun test to verify (Story 4.1)
Extend `src/handler/handler.test.ts` — test stdout output format, context injection enabled/disabled, failure path produces no stdout (AC: #1, #2, #3)

Svelte build pipeline tension: NFR-5 says no transpilation but Svelte needs build step
description: From Advanced Elicitation difficulty #5. Resolved by switching to Preact + htm (no build step). NFR-5 'no transpilation' applies to hook handler, not UI. If Preact isn't enough, build step at publish time (not install time) is acceptable. Linked to ch-0q3.

title: Register static file routes in `src/server/index.ts` — route `/` to `index.html`, route `/*.ts` and nested paths to the transpiler handler (AC: #1)

Write hookwatch design doc (goals, non-goals, FR, NFR)
description: Informed by observability repo analysis. Cover: goals, non-goals, functional requirements, non-functional requirements. Draw clear lines about what hookwatch does and doesn't do.
close_reason: All 5 open questions resolved. Design doc complete with 102 features classified, 18 events confirmed, key decisions locked.

Create `src/server/static.ts` — static file handler that serves files from `src/ui/`, transpiles `.ts` files on-the-fly via `Bun.Transpiler` with `Content-Type: application/javascript`, caches transpiled output in an in-memory `Map` keyed by file path + mtime (AC: #1)
Ship Claude Code skills as agent interface to hookwatch data
description: Instead of (or in addition to) a CLI, ship skills with the plugin that let the agent query hookwatch's SQLite database. Skills are more natural for agents than raw SQL. Examples: 'show recent events', 'filter by session', 'hook performance summary'. v1 feature.

Add README section: plugin system limitations and known issues
description: Add a section to README noting that the Claude Code plugin system currently has issues (reference GitHub issue number). Also mention that after v0 is ready, we'll invite more users to try the application.
notes: GitHub issue: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/28540 — Plugin system has known issues. Reference this in the README section.

title: Define `preToolUseOutputSchema` extending base with `hookSpecificOutput` containing `permissionDecision`, `updatedInput`, `additionalContext` (AC: #1)

Export inferred TypeScript types for all 18 event types (AC: #1)
Story 1.5: Server Auto-Start from Handler
description: As a Claude Code developer, I want the handler to start the Bun server automatically, so that event capture works without manual setup.

title: Write integration test — start server, send request before timeout, verify timer resets and server stays alive (AC: #2)

Create `src/cli/install.test.ts` — test plugin file generation (plugin.json validity, hooks.json contains all 18 event types with correct `bun <path>` command), install flow, reinstall idempotency, and uninstall cleanup (mock `claude` CLI calls). Scope: CLI and manifest only. Handler behavior and server startup are tested in Stories 1.4 and 1.5. (AC: #1, #2, #3)
Read version from `package.json` for `--version` flag (AC: #4)
Add expand/collapse signal per event row — toggle a per-row `expandedEventId` signal in event-list or a local signal in the row component (AC: #1, #3)

Apply `.passthrough()` on all schemas to preserve unknown fields (AC: #1, #2)
Create `src/schemas/output.ts` with base `hookOutputSchema` for generic hook output fields (AC: #1, #2)
Enforce parameterized SQL queries — no string concatenation
description: All SQLite queries must use parameterized queries (db.prepare().run()). Never concatenate user-provided values into SQL strings. Add this as an explicit rule in AGENTS.md. Prevents SQL injection via hook stdin payloads.

title: Implement browser open — after server is confirmed running, open the web UI URL in the default browser using `Bun.spawn(["open", url])` on macOS and `xdg-open` on Linux (AC: #1, #2)
Test: does SessionStart hook fire in non-interactive mode?
description: Verify whether the SessionStart hook fires when Claude Code runs in non-interactive mode (e.g., piped input, --print flag, SDK usage). If not, hookwatch may miss session starts in automated/scripted contexts. Test during implementation.

title: Modify `src/handler/index.ts` to build stdout output after successful POST to server (AC: #1, #3)
Implement best-effort capture — if server is unreachable after auto-start attempt, still pass through I/O to terminal (do not fail the wrapped command) (AC: #1, #3)
Write integration test — verify `hookwatch open` starts server when not running, opens browser, and handles already-running case (AC: #1, #2)
Create `src/ui/sessions/session-list.ts` — helper module for fetching and formatting session list data (AC: #1)
Run bunx biome check and bun test — verify no lint errors and all tests pass before marking story complete
Epic 2: Browse Events in Web UI
description: Developer opens a browser and sees a chronological event list with session filtering, event detail, and live updates via SSE. Includes hookwatch open for manual server start + browser launch and idle timeout for server lifecycle. FRs: FR10-13, FR15-16, FR21-22. NFRs: NFR3. Depends on Epic 1. Stories: 2.1, 2.2, 2.3, 2.4, 2.5, 2.6.

title: hookwatch open command (v0)
description: CLI command to manually start daemon + open browser. Entry point for browsing old data without waiting for a hook to fire. Identified during Party Mode target users discussion.

title: hookwatch wrap: Bun.spawn with pipe for stdin/stdout/stderr, no PTY
description: From Advanced Elicitation difficulty #6. Implementation approach: use Bun.spawn with stdin/stdout/stderr all set to 'pipe'. Read streams, forward to original destinations AND post to daemon. Simple pipe-based, no PTY emulation. If color issues arise, address later. Linked to ch-ek3.

title: Implement port auto-increment: try 6004, catch `EADDRINUSE`, increment, retry (AC: #4)
First Principles findings: mechanism vs need, 18/18 is infrastructure not value prop
description: From Advanced Elicitation First Principles. Key insights: (1) Developers need to understand agent behavior, hooks are the mechanism not the need. (2) 18/18 coverage is table-stakes infrastructure, not the value proposition users care about. (3) Real value is easiest path from 'what happened?' to 'I see it'. These inform messaging and positioning throughout docs.

title: Define Zod schemas for all 18 event types with event-specific fields (AC: #1)
Integrate spawn logic into `src/handler/index.ts` — trigger on `fetch()` connection refused (AC: #1, #3)
Ensure unknown event types pass through fallback schema and are forwarded to server (AC: #4)
Clone remaining 7 upstream repos
description: Clone the 7 remaining repos from tool-evaluation.md comparison table into .git-ignored/upstream/: TechNickAI/claude_telemetry, ColeMurray/claude-code-otel, connerohnesorge/conclaude, carlrannaberg/claudekit, Teraflop-Inc/dev-agent-lens, MacFall7/M87-Spine-lite. Low priority - the 4 most relevant are already cloned.

title: Two-plugin architecture: hooks plugin + skills plugin with shared backend
description: Split hookwatch into two Claude Code plugins sharing one backend. Plugin 1 (hooks, v0): event capture via handler.ts. Plugin 2 (skills, v1): agent-queryable skills. Both use the shared Bun backend (SQLite + web UI + wrap CLI). Reason: plugin system doesn't support partial installation. Design doc needs updating.

title: Wire `session-filter.ts` to `activeSession` signal — on selection change, update signal value (AC: #2)
Implement client-side session filter check — before adding an SSE event to `eventList`, check if `activeSession` signal is set and if the event's `session_id` matches (AC: #3)
Write unit test for idle timer — verify timer fires after configured duration with no activity, and resets when activity occurs (AC: #1, #2)
Create `src/schemas/output.test.ts` — tests for valid output passing, missing field rejection, strict mode rejecting unknown fields, systemMessage format validation (AC: #1, #2, #3)
Create `src/db/schema.test.ts` — test database creation, WAL mode, table existence, insert+retrieve round-trip, persistence after close+reopen (AC: #2, #3)
Define `activeSession` signal in `src/ui/app.ts` — shared state that drives filtering across components (AC: #2, #3)
Create `src/handler/index.ts` — single entry point for all hook events (AC: #1, #3, #4)
Evaluate agent-scoped guardrail rules (C4)
description: Decide whether hookwatch should support different guardrail rules per subagent type (e.g. stricter for untrusted agents). From conclaude. Guardrail category (v3).

title: Stub `open` and `wrap` subcommands (defined but not implemented — Epic 2 and Epic 3) (AC: #4)
Story 1.3: Bun Server with Event Ingestion Endpoint
description: As a hookwatch handler, I want a Bun server that accepts events via HTTP, so that captured events flow from handler to database.

title: Implement server start logic — if server not running, spawn Bun server as a detached background process using the `spawnServer` function from `src/handler/spawn.ts`, then poll `GET /health` until ready (AC: #1)
Standalone desktop app (Electron/Tauri)
description: Wrap hookwatch web UI as a standalone desktop app. Evaluate Electron vs Tauri (Rust, smaller binary). Requires the web UI to be mature enough. Consider if all features (SSE live updates, SQLite access) work in a desktop app context.

title: Define `userPromptSubmitOutputSchema` extending base with `hookSpecificOutput` containing `additionalContext` (AC: #1)
Create `src/server/server.test.ts` — test health endpoint, successful event ingestion, invalid JSON rejection, port auto-increment (AC: #1, #2, #3, #4)

Implement server detection — read port from `~/.claude/hookwatch/hookwatch.port`; if file exists, attempt `GET /health` on that port; if file absent or health check fails, server is not running (AC: #2)
Forward child process exit code as the hookwatch wrap exit code (AC: #2)
Integrate broadcast into `src/server/ingest.ts` — after successful event insertion into SQLite, call `broadcast(event)` to push to all SSE clients (AC: #1)
Create `src/db/connection.ts` — open database, create parent directory, set 0600 permissions immediately after file creation, enable WAL mode (AC: #2)
Story 2.4: SSE Live Updates
description: As a Claude Code developer, I want the event list to update in real-time as new events arrive, so that I can watch agent activity live.

title: Create `src/schemas/events.ts` with common fields schema shared by all events (AC: #1, #3)
Story 2.6: Server Idle Timeout
description: As a Claude Code developer, I want the Bun server to self-terminate after inactivity, so that it doesn't consume resources when I'm not using it.

title: Phase 3: QA & Hardening
description: Multi-round quality assurance after implementation. Integration testing, performance validation (NFR-1: <100ms), edge cases, dogfooding hookwatch on itself.

title: Git-based file change tracking per tool call
description: Use git to track which tool call changed which file by snapshotting git state between tool calls. Requires git (no git = no feature). jj support deferred further. Harder feature — v2. If not enough users by v1, project may be discontinued before this ships.

title: Export inferred TypeScript types: `HookOutput`, `PreToolUseOutput`, `UserPromptSubmitOutput` (AC: #1)
Run `bun init` to generate `package.json` and `tsconfig.json` (AC: #1)
Prevent XSS: never use innerHTML/dangerouslySetInnerHTML
description: htm auto-escapes by default, but payload rendering (JSON viewer, event detail) must never bypass this. Add explicit rule in AGENTS.md: no innerHTML, no dangerouslySetInnerHTML. All payload rendering goes through htm's tagged template escaping.

title: Register `open` subcommand in `src/cli/index.ts` — add to citty main command (AC: #1)
Story 1.2: Zod Event Validation Schemas
description: As a hookwatch handler, I want Zod schemas for all 18 event types, so that incoming stdin payloads are validated with strict known fields and permissive unknown fields.

title: User persona insights: power user wants filters, plugin dev wants wrap, casual user wants human-readable labels
description: From Advanced Elicitation User Persona Focus Group. Power user: filter panel matters more than event count. Plugin developer: v0 has limited value without wrap feature (v1). Casual user: web UI needs human-readable labels (e.g. 'Claude read this file' not 'PreToolUse'). No action required now — inform v0 and v1 UI design.

title: Create `tests/handler-server.test.ts` — integration test for full handler -> spawn -> health probe -> POST -> delivery cycle (AC: #1, #2, #3, #4)
Run Biome lint + bun test to verify (Story 4.2)
Epic 4: Context Injection
description: Hook handler outputs structured system messages to Claude Code via stdout, identifying the source hook and event type. Includes bidirectional Zod validation of hook stdout. FRs: FR27-28. Depends on Epic 1. Stories: 4.1, 4.2.

title: Implement `Bun.spawn()` with `detached: true` to run server as background process (AC: #1)
Create `AGENTS.md` with all project conventions: ch-lar (parameterized SQL only), ch-u88 (no innerHTML), snake_case DB/API fields, camelCase TS, kebab-case files, Biome linting (AC: #1)
Integrate wrap-viewer into event detail expansion — when event type is "Wrap", render wrap-viewer instead of the standard event detail view (AC: #1, #2)
Insert new events at the beginning of the `eventList` signal array — maintains reverse-chronological order without re-fetching (AC: #1)
Implement explicit port error — when `--port` is specified and the port is occupied (either by hookwatch or another process), display error "port {N} in use" and exit 1 (AC: #4)
Create `src/ui/events/event-detail.ts` — Preact component that receives an event object and renders the expanded detail view (AC: #1, #2)
Implement stderr panel — <pre><code> block displaying captured stderr text (AC: #2)
Validate output against stdout schema (Story 4.1) before writing to stdout (AC: #3)
Verify `bun test` passes and server serves UI correctly from clean state (AC: #1, #2, #3)
Display exit code with color coding — green for exit code 0, red for non-zero (AC: #2)
Create `src/server/query.ts` — `POST /api/query` handler that accepts a filter object, validates with `src/schemas/query.ts`, queries `src/db/queries.ts`, returns JSON array of events (AC: #2)
Create `src/ui/sessions/session-filter.ts` — Preact component that fetches distinct session IDs on mount and renders a `<select>` dropdown with "All sessions" as the default option (AC: #1, #3)
Remove 'offline-first' as differentiator, replace with 'zero-config'
description: Offline is table stakes for a local tool, not a differentiator. Replace with zero-config as the positioning.

title: Event filtering and per-hook log control
description: v1: Allow users to configure which event types are captured and which fields are stored or redacted. Per-hook enable/disable of logging. Per-event-type field filtering (e.g., redact tool_input for Bash events). Part of the v1 setup wizard.

title: Create `src/ui/index.html` — shell HTML that loads Pico CSS from CDN (`<link>`), loads `app.ts` as `<script type="module">` (AC: #1)
Service worker + UI restart button for offline Bun server
description: Cache web UI static assets via service worker so the page loads even when Bun server is down. Show 'server offline' state with a restart button in the UI. v1 feature.

title: Write validated JSON to stdout and exit 0 on success (AC: #1, #3)
Wire idle timer reset into the request handler — add `resetIdleTimer()` call at the top of the Bun.serve fetch handler so every HTTP request resets the timer (AC: #2)
Bind server to `127.0.0.1` explicitly — never `0.0.0.0` (AC: #1)
Define `hooks.json` structure with all 18 event types registered using `".*"` matcher (AC: #1)
Create `src/server/health.ts` — `GET /health` handler returning `200 OK` (AC: #1)
Write unit test for port detection logic — verify health check correctly identifies running/not-running server (AC: #2)
Implement stdin forwarding — pipe process.stdin to child process stdin (AC: #1)
Create `src/schemas/query.ts` — Zod schema for the query filter object (AC: #2)
Implement empty state rendering in `event-list.ts` — display a message when the event list is empty (AC: #3)
